boards.name: CBSE
boards.overview: Widely followed across India for school education and board examinations in Classes 10 and 12.

boards.name: ICSE
boards.overview: Known for a detailed syllabus, strong English language focus, and balanced academics.

boards.name: State Board
boards.overview: Each state follows its own syllabus, language policy, and examination structure.

boards.name: Regional State Board
boards.overview: State-board students should follow the official syllabus, local assessment rules, and exam schedule issued by their board.

policies.title: Attendance Requirement
policies.summary: Many Indian schools require around 75% attendance for board exam eligibility.
policies.details: Students should keep medical documents and school approvals ready when attendance falls below the threshold. Always check the school handbook for board-specific rules.

policies.title: Assessment Pattern
policies.summary: Marks typically come from periodic tests, term exams, practicals, and final board preparation.
policies.details: CBSE schools often use competency-based questions and internal assessment. ICSE schools include project work and written exams. State boards may vary by state and class.

policies.title: Promotion and Re-examination
policies.summary: Promotion rules depend on the school board, class level, and internal assessment performance.
policies.details: Students should follow school circulars for supplementary exams, re-evaluation, and subject improvement options.

policies.title: Academic Integrity
policies.summary: Copying in schoolwork, projects, or board exams can lead to disciplinary action.
policies.details: Students should submit original work, cite sources in projects, and follow exam hall rules carefully.

courses.name: Class 10 Mathematics Foundation
courses.description: Algebra, geometry, trigonometry basics, statistics, and real-life problem solving for board-prep revision.

courses.name: Class 12 Physics Foundation
courses.description: Mechanics, electricity, optics, modern physics, and practical-based revision for board exams.

courses.name: Class 10 English and Writing Foundation
courses.description: Grammar, literature, writing skills, comprehension, and language development for board-prep revision.

courses.name: State Board Science Foundation
courses.description: Foundational science topics aligned to state board textbooks and exam preparation.

programs.title: Diploma in Computer Engineering
programs.best_for: students who want a hands-on technical route after Class 10 or Class 12
programs.note: Check the current admission brochure and intake seats for the latest academic year.

programs.title: Diploma in Pharmacy (D.Pharm)
programs.best_for: students interested in health sciences, laboratories, and pharmacy careers
institutions: Government pharmacy colleges in multiple states
programs.note: Admission rules vary by institution; verify PCI approval and seat availability before applying.

programs.title: B.Sc. in Computer Science / Data Science
programs.best_for: students who want a science-based undergraduate route into software, analytics, or postgraduate study
institutions: University of Delhi colleges such as Hansraj College and Kirori Mal College
programs.note: Course titles and eligibility differ by college; confirm whether the college offers a B.Sc. or BCA track.

programs.title: B.Com. / B.Com. (Hons.)
programs.best_for: students interested in accounting, business, banking, finance, and professional courses
institutions: Shri Ram College of Commerce, Delhi University
programs.note: Many colleges require Class 12 mathematics or strong commerce subjects for honours programs.

programs.title: B.A. in Psychology / Humanities
programs.best_for: students who enjoy writing, social sciences, public policy, or psychology
institutions: Lady Shri Ram College for Women, Delhi
Symbiosis College of Arts and Commerce, Pune
programs.note: Admission may depend on merit, entrance tests, or both depending on the institution.

programs.title: MBA / PGDM
programs.best_for: graduates who want careers in management, consulting, operations, marketing, or entrepreneurship
programs.note: Admission usually depends on entrance tests, work-experience preferences, and interview rounds.

programs.title: M.Sc. in Data Science / Computer Science
programs.best_for: graduates aiming for analytics, research, software engineering, or higher study
programs.note: Check program specialization, entrance requirements, and department intake for the current cycle.

class_11.decision: For engineering, choose Science - Non-medical in Class 11 rather than medical, commerce, or humanities.
class_11.focus: Build strong math and physics fundamentals, keep chemistry steady, and start coding/problem-solving practice early.

what_to_do: Target Class 12 board score consistency in Maths, Physics, and Chemistry.
Prepare for JEE, state CETs, or institution-level entrances where relevant.
Create a project portfolio with coding, robotics, or lab work examples.

diploma: Baba Banda Singh Bahadur Engineering College, Fatehgarh Sahib (near Ludhiana)
undergraduate: Baba Banda Singh Bahadur Engineering College, Fatehgarh Sahib
postgraduate: Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology, Patiala

avg_fees.undergraduate: ₹1,50,000 - ₹4,00,000 per year for private colleges; ₹80,000 - ₹2,00,000 for good state/university colleges
avg_fees.postgraduate: ₹1,50,000 - ₹4,00,000 per year depending on institute and specialization

class_12.salary_overview: Engineering careers in 2026-27 typically begin at ₹30,000 - ₹60,000 per month for fresh graduates, with strong growth into ₹1,00,000 - ₹1,50,000+ for experienced engineers and ₹2,00,000+ for top roles.

class_11.decision: For medical pathways, choose Science - Medical in Class 11; avoid non-medical, commerce, or humanities if MBBS/BDS is your goal.
class_11.focus: Keep biology as the main anchor, strengthen chemistry, and maintain disciplined practical and theory revision.

what_to_do: Study NCERT line by line for Biology, Chemistry, and Physics.
Plan NEET-style practice, diagram work, and timed revision.
Shortlist medical, allied health, or research pathways early.

institutions: Government medical institutes in multiple states

diploma: Dayanand Medical College & Hospital, Ludhiana (DPT, BPT allied health)
undergraduate: Sri Guru Ram Das Institute of Medical Sciences & Research, Amritsar

avg_fees.undergraduate: ₹2,00,000 - ₹7,00,000 per year for private medical colleges; ₹50,000 - ₹3,00,000 for government institutions
avg_fees.postgraduate: ₹3,00,000 - ₹12,00,000 per year depending on specialization and institute

class_12.salary_overview: Medical and allied health careers in 2026-27 typically start at ₹40,000 - ₹90,000 per month for fresh graduates, with specialist doctors and consultants earning ₹1,50,000+ per month.

class_11.decision: For commerce and management, choose Commerce in Class 11 with Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, and optional Mathematics if possible.
class_11.focus: Build basics in accounts, business concepts, and economics; keep communication and spreadsheet skills strong.

what_to_do: Prepare for board exams with strong numeracy and concept clarity.
Explore CUET, CA Foundation, CS Foundation, or B.Com-related admission routes.
Develop practical skills in MS Excel, presentation, and basic analytics.

institutions: Polytechnic and vocational institutes across states

avg_fees.undergraduate: ₹1,00,000 - ₹3,50,000 per year for private colleges; ₹60,000 - ₹1,50,000 for strong public colleges
avg_fees.postgraduate: ₹1,50,000 - ₹6,00,000 per year depending on program and institute

class_12.salary_overview: Commerce and management careers in 2026-27 generally start at ₹30,000 - ₹55,000 per month, with strong performers in finance/consulting reaching ₹80,000+ early on.

class_11.decision: For Humanities, choose Humanities in Class 11 and avoid Science or Commerce if you want strong psychology/public policy options.
class_11.focus: Strengthen writing, reading, analysis, and subject reasoning while exploring the careers that need communication and social understanding.

what_to_do: Keep essays, case studies, and answer writing crisp and well structured.
Use CUET or university-specific entrances where required.
Build reading, debate, and research habits to support university applications.

avg_fees.undergraduate: ₹80,000 - ₹2,50,000 per year for private colleges; ₹40,000 - ₹1,00,000 for strong public colleges
avg_fees.postgraduate: ₹70,000 - ₹2,50,000 per year depending on program and institute

class_12.salary_overview: Humanities careers in 2026-27 generally start at ₹25,000 - ₹50,000 per month for entry-level roles, rising to ₹60,000 - ₹1,20,000+ in policy, research, media, or analytics jobs.

regional_resources.label: Metro city study plan
regional_resources.tips: Use the stronger coaching ecosystem for regular testing, concept revision, and timed sample papers. Keep a disciplined weekly schedule and track weak chapters.

regional_resources.label: Tier-2 city study plan
regional_resources.tips: Balance school, self-study, and travel time carefully. Focus on board syllabus completion early, then use weekends for revision and doubt clearing.

regional_resources.label: Small town study plan
regional_resources.tips: Rely on textbook mastery, online classes, and peer study groups. Keep notes simple, revise often, and practise sample papers under timed conditions.

regional_resources.label: School support plan
regional_resources.tips: Build regular contact with teachers, ask for extra practice material early, and keep attendance and homework records organised for board preparation.
